etymology.description: Pictogram of bow firing arrow -> hand 攴 added -> hand evolved to 癹 (phonetic) -> final form 發
etymology.interpretation: 發 initially depicted a bow firing an arrow, symbolizing the idea of emitting or releasing. The added hand component indicated an action, aligning with the sense of 'issuing or emitting'. With 癹 serving the phonetic purpose, it unified into the character 發, emphasizing the concept of sending forth or dispatching, as in 'emit' or 'issue'.
etymology.simplification: 发 is the simplified form of 發, reducing the complex 癹 component into a simpler structure while retaining pronunciation.

examples.chinese: 公司明天会发布新产品(公司明天會發布新產品)。
examples.pinyin: Gōngsī míngtiān huì fābù xīn chǎnpǐn.
examples.english: The company will issue a new product tomorrow.

examples.chinese: 她的表演发出耀眼的光芒(她的表演發出耀眼的光芒)。
examples.pinyin: Tā de biǎoyǎn fāchū yàoyǎn de guāngmáng.
examples.english: Her performance emitted a dazzling light.

examples.chinese: 请尽快发送邮件(請盡快發送郵件)。
examples.english: Please send the email as soon as possible.
